fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>x</code>: One or more expressions or columns (Tuple, Array, Date, DateTime, String, or numeric types).</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>The approximate number of distinct values as a <code>UInt64</code>.</p><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">SELECT uniq(number % 10) FROM numbers(100)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─uniq(modulo(number, 10))─┐
│                       10 │
└──────────────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="uniqcombined" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">uniqCombined<a class="headerlink" href="#uniqcombined" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Calculates the approximate number of distinct argument values using a combination of array, hash table, and HyperLogLog algorithms. It offers better memory efficiency and accuracy than <code>uniq</code> but can be slightly slower.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">uniqCombined(HLL_precision)(x[, ...])
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="parameters" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Parameters<a class="headerlink" href="#parameters" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>HLL_precision</code>: An optional <code>UInt64</code> (base-2 logarithm of HyperLogLog cells). Default: 17.</li></ul><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>x</code>: One or more expressions or columns (Tuple, Array, Date, DateTime, String, or numeric types).</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>The approximate number of distinct values as a <code>UInt64</code>.</p><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">SELECT uniqCombined(number) FROM numbers(1e6)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─uniqCombined(number)─┐
│              1001148 │
└──────────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="uniqcombined64" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">uniqCombined64<a class="headerlink" href="#uniqcombined64" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Calculates the approximate number of distinct argument values, similar to <code>uniqCombined</code>, but uses a 64-bit hash for all data types. This provides higher accuracy for very large cardinalities, especially for non-string types.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">uniqCombined64(HLL_precision)(x[, ...])
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="parameters" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Parameters<a class="headerlink" href="#parameters" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>HLL_precision</code>: An optional <code>UInt64</code> (base-2 logarithm of HyperLogLog cells). Default: 17.</li></ul><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>x</code>: One or more expressions or columns (Tuple, Array, Date, DateTime, String, or numeric types).</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>The approximate number of distinct values as a <code>UInt64</code>.</p><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">SELECT uniqCombined64(number) FROM numbers(1e6)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─uniqCombined64(number)─┐
│                1000000 │
└────────────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="uniqexact" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">uniqExact<a class="headerlink" href="#uniqexact" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Calculates the exact number of distinct argument values. This function consumes <code>O(n)</code> memory, where <code>n</code> is the number of distinct values, and should be used when absolute precision is required.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">uniqExact(x[, ...])
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>x</code>: One or more expressions or columns (Tuple, Array, Date, DateTime, String, or numeric types).</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>The exact number of distinct values as a <code>UInt64</code>.</p><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">SELECT uniqExact(number % 10) FROM numbers(100)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─uniqExact(modulo(number, 10))─┐
│                            10 │
└───────────────────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="uniqhll12" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">uniqHLL12<a class="headerlink" href="#uniqhll12" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Calculates the approximate number of distinct argument values using the HyperLogLog algorithm with 2^12 5-bit cells. This function is generally not recommended for new development due to its lower accuracy compared to <code>uniq</code> or <code>uniqCombined</code>.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">uniqHLL12(x[, ...])
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>x</code>: One or more expressions or columns (Tuple, Array, Date, DateTime, String, or numeric types).</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>The approximate number of distinct values as a <code>UInt64</code>.</p><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">SELECT uniqHLL12(number) FROM numbers(1000)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─uniqHLL12(number)─┐
│               999 │
└───────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="uniqtheta" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">uniqTheta<a class="headerlink" href="#uniqtheta" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Calculates the approximate number of distinct argument values using the Theta Sketch Framework (KMV algorithm). It provides a relative error of 3.125% (95% confidence).</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">uniqTheta(x[, ...])
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>x</code>: One or more expressions or columns (Tuple, Array, Date, DateTime, String, or numeric types).</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>The approximate number of distinct values as a <code>UInt64</code>.</p><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">SELECT uniqTheta(number) FROM numbers(1000)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─uniqTheta(number)─┐
│              1000 │
└───────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="varpop" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">varPop<a class="headerlink" href="#varpop" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Calculates the population variance of a set of numeric values. This function uses a numerically unstable algorithm.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">varPop(x)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Alias: <code>VAR_POP</code></p><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>x</code>: A numeric expression (Integer, Float, or Decimal).</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>The population variance as a <code>Float64</code>.</p><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">SELECT varPop(number) FROM numbers(10)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─varPop(number)─┐
│           8.25 │
└────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="varpopstable" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">varPopStable<a class="headerlink" href="#varpopstable" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Calculates the population variance of a set of numeric values using a numerically stable algorithm. This function is generally slower but provides more accurate results than <code>varPop</code>.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">varPopStable(x)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Alias: <code>VAR_POP_STABLE</code></p><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>x</code>: A numeric expression (Integer, Float, or Decimal).</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>The population variance as a <code>Float64</code>.</p><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">SELECT varPopStable(number) FROM numbers(10)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─varPopStable(number)─┐
│                 8.25 │
└──────────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="varsamp" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">varSamp<a class="headerlink" href="#varsamp" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Calculates the sample variance of a set of numeric values. This function uses a numerically unstable algorithm.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">varSamp(x)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Alias: <code>VAR_SAMP</code></p><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>x</code>: A numeric expression (Integer, Float, or Decimal).</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>The sample variance as a <code>Float64</code>.</p><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">SELECT varSamp(number) FROM numbers(10)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─varSamp(number)─┐
│            9.166666666666666 │
└─────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="varsampstable" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">varSampStable<a class="headerlink" href="#varsampstable" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Calculates the sample variance of a set of numeric values using a numerically stable algorithm. This function is generally slower but provides more accurate results than <code>varSamp</code>.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">varSampStable(x)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Alias: <code>VAR_SAMP_STABLE</code></p><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>x</code>: A numeric expression (Integer, Float, or Decimal).</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>The sample variance as a <code>Float64</code>.</p><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">SELECT varSampStable(number) FROM numbers(10)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─varSampStable(number)─┐
│    9.166666666666666 │
└───────────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="welchttest" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">welchTTest<a class="headerlink" href="#welchttest" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Applies Welch&#x27;s t-test to compare two samples and determine if their population means are equal, without assuming equal variances.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">welchTTest([confidence_level])(sample_data, sample_index)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="parameters" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Parameters<a class="headerlink" href="#parameters" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>confidence_level</code>: An optional <code>Float64</code> confidence level for calculating confidence intervals.</li></ul><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>sample_data</code>: The numeric values from both samples (Integer, Float, or Decimal).</li><li><code>sample_index</code>: An <code>Integer</code> (0 or 1) indicating which sample the <code>sample_data</code> belongs to.</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>A tuple containing the calculated t-statistic and p-value. If <code>confidence_level</code> is specified, it also includes the lower and upper bounds of the confidence interval. Tuple(Float64, Float64) or Tuple(Float64, Float64, Float64, Float64).</p><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">WITH welch_ttest AS (
  SELECT arrayJoin([(20.3,0), (22.1,0), (21.9,0), (18.9,1), (20.3,1), (19.0,1)]) AS data_pair
)
SELECT welchTTest(data_pair.1, data_pair.2) FROM welch_ttest
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─welchTTest(data_pair.1, data_pair.2)──────┐
│ (2.7988719532211235,0.051807360348581945) │
└───────────────────────────────────────────┘

Aggregate Functions reference\nmeta:\n    description: Functions for performing calculations on a set of values and return a single value.\nheadingMaxLevels: 2\n---\n\n# Aggregate functions\n\nAggregate functions perform a calculation on a set of values and return a single value. They are commonly used in data analysis and reporting to summarize large datasets. These functions can be used to compute sums, averages, counts, and other statistical measures.\n\nDuring aggregation, `NULL` arguments are generally skipped. If an aggregate function has multiple arguments, any row where one or more of these arguments are `NULL` will be ignored. Some functions, like `first_value` or `last_value` with the `RESPECT NULLS` modifier, are exceptions to this rule.\n\n## Aggregate function combinators\n\nThe name of an aggregate function can have a suffix appended to it. This changes the way the aggregate function works.\n\n### -If\n\nThe suffix -If can be appended to the name of any aggregate function. In this case, the aggregate function accepts an extra argument – a condition (Uint8 type). The aggregate function processes only the rows that trigger the condition. If the condition was not triggered even once, it returns a default value (usually zeros or empty strings).\n\nExamples: `sumIf(column, cond)`, `countIf(cond)`, `avgIf(x, cond)`, `quantilesTimingIf(level1, level2)(x, cond)`, `argMinIf(arg, val, cond)` and so on.\n\nWith conditional aggregate functions, you can calculate aggregates for several conditions at once, without using subqueries and `JOIN`s. For example, conditional aggregate functions can be used to implement the segment comparison functionality.\n\n### -Array\n\nThe -Array suffix can be appended to any aggregate function. In this case, the aggregate function takes arguments of the 'Array(T)' type (arrays) instead of 'T' type arguments. If the aggregate function accepts multiple arguments, this must be arrays of equal lengths. When processing arrays, the aggregate function works like the original aggregate function across all array elements.\n\nExample 1: `sumArray(arr)` - Totals all the elements of all 'arr' arrays. In this example, it could have been written more simply: `sum(arraySum(arr))`.\n\nExample 2: `uniqArray(arr)` – Counts the number of unique elements in all 'arr' arrays. This could be done an easier way: `uniq(arrayJoin(arr))`, but it's not always possible to add 'arrayJoin' to a query.\n\n-If and -Array can be combined. However, 'Array' must come first, then 'If'. Examples: `uniqArrayIf(arr, cond)`, `quantilesTimingArrayIf(level1, level2)(arr, cond)`. Due to this order, the 'cond' argument won't be an array.\n\n### -Map\n\nThe -Map suffix can be appended to any aggregate function. This will create an aggregate function which gets Map type as an argument, and aggregates values of each key of the map separately using the specified aggregate function. The result is also of a Map type.\n\n#### Example\n\n```sql\nWITH map_map AS (\n  SELECT c1::Date AS date, c2::DateTime AS timeslot, c3::Map(String, UInt16) AS status FROM values (\n    ('2000-01-01', '2000-01-01 00:00:00', (['a', 'b', 'c'], [10, 10, 10])),\n    ('2000-01-01', '2000-01-01 00:00:00', (['c', 'd', 'e'], [10, 10, 10])),\n    ('2000-01-01', '2000-01-01 00:01:00', (['d', 'e', 'f'], [10, 10, 10])),\n    ('2000-01-01', '2000-01-01 00:01:00', (['f', 'g', 'g'], [10, 10, 10]))\n))\nSELECT\n    timeslot,\n    sumMap(status),\n    avgMap(status),\n    minMap(status)\nFROM map_map\nGROUP BY timeslot\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌────────────timeslot─┬─sumMap(status)───────────────────────┬─avgMap(status)───────────────────────┬─minMap(status)───────────────────────┐\n│ 2000-01-01 00:00:00 │ {'a':10,'b':10,'c':20,'d':10,'e':10} │ {'a':10,'b':10,'c':10,'d':10,'e':10} │ {'a':10,'b':10,'c':10,'d':10,'e':10} │\n│ 2000-01-01 00:01:00 │ {'d':10,'e':10,'f':20,'g':20}        │ {'d':10,'e':10,'f':10,'g':10}        │ {'d':10,'e':10,'f':10,'g':10}        │\n└─────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n### -SimpleState\n\nIf you apply this combinator, the aggregate function returns the same value but with a different type. This is a SimpleAggregateFunction(...) that can be stored in a table to work with AggregatingMergeTree tables.\n\n#### Syntax\n\n```sql\n\u003caggFunction\u003eSimpleState(x)\n```\n\n#### Arguments\n\n- `x`: Aggregate function parameters.\n\n#### Returned values\n\nThe value of an aggregate function with the `SimpleAggregateFunction(...)` type.\n\n#### Example\n\nQuery:\n\n```sql\nWITH anySimpleState(number) AS c SELECT toTypeName(c), c FROM numbers(1)\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─toTypeName(c)────────────────────────┬─c─┐\n│ SimpleAggregateFunction(any, UInt64) │ 0 │\n└──────────────────────────────────────┴───┘\n```\n\n### -State\n\nIf you apply this combinator, the aggregate function doesn't return the resulting value (such as the number of unique values for the uniq function), but an intermediate state of the aggregation (for `uniq`, this is the hash table for calculating the number of unique values). This is an `AggregateFunction(...)` that can be used for further processing or stored in a table to finish aggregating later.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\n`-MapState` isn't an invariant for the same data because the order of data in intermediate state can change, though it doesn't impact ingestion of this data.\n{% /callout %}\n\nTo work with these states, use:\n\n- AggregatingMergeTree table engine.\n- finalizeAggregation function.\n- runningAccumulate function.\n- -Merge combinator.\n- -MergeState combinator.\n\n### -Merge\n\nIf you apply this combinator, the aggregate function takes the intermediate aggregation state as an argument, combines the states to finish aggregation, and returns the resulting value.\n\n### -MergeState\n\nMerges the intermediate aggregation states in the same way as the -Merge combinator. However, it doesn't return the resulting value, but an intermediate aggregation state, similar to the -State combinator.\n\n### -ForEach\n\nConverts an aggregate function for tables into an aggregate function for arrays that aggregates the corresponding array items and returns an array of results. For example, `sumForEach` for the arrays `[1, 2]`, `[3, 4, 5]`and`[6, 7]`returns the result `[10, 13, 5]` after adding together the corresponding array items.\n\n### -Distinct\n\nEvery unique combination of arguments will be aggregated only once. Repeating values are ignored.\nExamples: `sum(DISTINCT x)` (or `sumDistinct(x)`), `groupArray(DISTINCT x)` (or `groupArrayDistinct(x)`), `corrStable(DISTINCT x, y)` (or `corrStableDistinct(x, y)`) and so on.\n\n### -OrDefault\n\nChanges behavior of an aggregate function.\n\nIf an aggregate function doesn't have input values, with this combinator it returns the default value for its return data type. Applies to the aggregate functions that can take empty input data.\n\n`-OrDefault` can be used with other combinators.\n\n#### Syntax\n\n```sql\n\u003caggFunction\u003eOrDefault(x)\n```\n\n#### Arguments\n\n- `x`: Aggregate function parameters.\n\n#### Returned values\n\nReturns the default value of an aggregate function's return type if there is nothing to aggregate.\n\nType depends on the aggregate function used.\n\n#### Example\n\nQuery:\n\n```sql\nSELECT avg(number), avgOrDefault(number) FROM numbers(0)\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─avg(number)─-─avgOrDefault(number)─┐\n│         nan │                    0 │\n└─────────────┴──────────────────────┘\n```\n\nAlso `-OrDefault` can be used with another combinators. It is useful when the aggregate function doesn't accept the empty input.\n\nQuery:\n\n```sql\nSELECT avgOrDefaultIf(x, x \u003e 10)\nFROM\n(\n    SELECT toDecimal32(1.23, 2) AS x\n)\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─avgOrDefaultIf(x, greater(x, 10))─┐\n│                              0.00 │\n└───────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n### -OrNull\n\nChanges behavior of an aggregate function.\n\nThis combinator converts a result of an aggregate function to the Nullable data type. If the aggregate function doesn't have values to calculate it returns NULL.\n\n`-OrNull` can be used with other combinators.\n\n#### Syntax\n\n```sql\n\u003caggFunction\u003eOrNull(x)\n```\n\n#### Arguments\n\n- `x`: Aggregate function parameters.\n\n### Returned values\n\n- The result of the aggregate function, converted to the `Nullable` data type.\n- `NULL`, if there is nothing to aggregate.\n\nType: `Nullable(aggregate function return type)`.\n\n#### Example\n\nAdd `-orNull` to the end of aggregate function.\n\nQuery:\n\n```sql\nSELECT sumOrNull(number), toTypeName(sumOrNull(number)) FROM numbers(10) WHERE number \u003e 10\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─sumOrNull(number)─┬─toTypeName(sumOrNull(number))─┐\n│              ᴺᵁᴸᴸ │ Nullable(UInt64)              │\n└───────────────────┴───────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\nAlso `-OrNull` can be used with another combinators. It is useful when the aggregate function doesn't accept the empty input.\n\nQuery:\n\n```sql\nSELECT avgOrNullIf(x, x \u003e 10)\nFROM\n(\n    SELECT toDecimal32(1.23, 2) AS x\n)\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─avgOrNullIf(x, greater(x, 10))─┐\n│                           ᴺᵁᴸᴸ │\n└────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n### -Resample\n\nLets you divide data into groups, and then separately aggregates the data in those groups. Groups are created by splitting the values from one column into intervals.\n\n```sql\n\u003caggFunction\u003eResample(start, end, step)(\u003caggFunction_params\u003e, resampling_key)\n```\n\n#### Arguments\n\n- `start`: Starting value of the whole required interval for `resampling_key` values.\n- `stop`: Ending value of the whole required interval for `resampling_key` values. The whole interval doesn't include the `stop` value `[start, stop)`.\n- `step`: Step for separating the whole interval into subintervals. The `aggFunction` is executed over each of those subintervals independently.\n- `resampling_key`: Column whose values are used for separating data into intervals.\n- `aggFunction_params`: `aggFunction` parameters.\n\n#### Returned values\n\n- Array of `aggFunction` results for each subinterval.\n\n#### Example\n\nConsider the `people` table with the following data:\n\n```text\n┌─name───┬─age─┬─wage─┐\n│ John   │  16 │   10 │\n│ Alice  │  30 │   15 │\n│ Mary   │  35 │    8 │\n│ Evelyn │  48 │ 11.5 │\n│ David  │  62 │  9.9 │\n│ Brian  │  60 │   16 │\n└────────┴─────┴──────┘\n```\n\nLet's get the names of the people whose age lies in the intervals of `[30,60)` and `[60,75)`. Since we use integer representation for age, we get ages in the `[30, 59]` and `[60,74]` intervals.\n\nTo aggregate names in an array, we use the groupArray aggregate function. It takes one argument. In our case, it's the `name` column. The `groupArrayResample` function should use the `age` column to aggregate names by age. To define the required intervals, we pass the `30, 75, 30` arguments into the `groupArrayResample` function.\n\n```sql\nSELECT groupArrayResample(30, 75, 30)(name, age) FROM people\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─groupArrayResample(30, 75, 30)(name, age)─────┐\n│ [['Alice','Mary','Evelyn'],['David','Brian']] │\n└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\nConsider the results.\n\n`John` is out of the sample because he's too young. Other people are distributed according to the specified age intervals.\n\nNow let's count the total number of people and their average wage in the specified age intervals.\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    countResample(30, 75, 30)(name, age) AS amount,\n    avgResample(30, 75, 30)(wage, age) AS avg_wage\nFROM people\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─amount─┬─avg_wage──────────────────┐\n│ [3,2]  │ [11.5,12.949999809265137] │\n└────────┴───────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n### -ArgMin\n\nThe suffix -ArgMin can be appended to the name of any aggregate function. In this case, the aggregate function accepts an additional argument, which should be any comparable expression. The aggregate function processes only the rows that have the minimum value for the specified extra expression.\n\nExamples: `sumArgMin(column, expr)`, `countArgMin(expr)`, `avgArgMin(x, expr)` and so on.\n\n### -ArgMax\n\nSimilar to suffix -ArgMin but processes only the rows that have the maximum value for the specified extra expression.\n\n## analysisOfVariance\n\nPerforms a one-way analysis of variance (ANOVA) test to determine if the means of two or more groups are significantly different.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\nanalysisOfVariance(val, group_no)\n```\n\nAlias: `anova`\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `val`: Numeric value for the observation.\n- `group_no`: Integer representing the group the `val` belongs to. Groups are enumerated starting from 0.\n\n### Returns\n\nA tuple containing the F-statistic and the p-value of the ANOVA test. Tuple(Float64, Float64).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT analysisOfVariance(number, number % 2) FROM numbers(100)\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─analysisOfVariance(number, modulo(number, 2))─┐\n│ (0,1)                                         │\n└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## any\n\nSelects the first encountered non-`NULL` value from a column within a group. The order of selection is non-deterministic unless the data is explicitly ordered.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\nany(column) [RESPECT NULLS]\n```\n\nAliases: `any_value`, `first_value`.\nFor `any(column) RESPECT NULLS`: `any_respect_nulls`, `first_value_respect_nulls`, `any_value_respect_nulls`.\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `column`: The column from which to select a value.\n\n### Returns\n\nThe first value encountered. If no non-`NULL` values are found, it returns the default value for the column's type (e.g., 0 for integers, empty string for strings). If `RESPECT NULLS` is used, it returns the first value encountered, even if it's `NULL`. The return type matches the input column's type.\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nWITH cte AS (SELECT arrayJoin([NULL, 'Amsterdam', 'New York', 'Tokyo', 'Valencia', NULL]) as city)\nSELECT any(city), any_respect_nulls(city) FROM cte\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─any(city)─┬─any_respect_nulls(city)─┐\n│ Amsterdam │ ᴺᵁᴸᴸ                    │\n└───────────┴─────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## anyHeavy\n\nIdentifies a frequently occurring value in a column using the heavy hitters algorithm. This function is non-deterministic and aims to find a value that appears in more than half of the cases within each execution thread.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\nanyHeavy(column)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `column`: The column from which to find a frequently occurring value.\n\n### Returns\n\nA frequently occurring value from the column. The return type matches the input column's type.\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nWITH cte AS (SELECT arrayJoin([2,1,1,1,3,1,1,2,2]) as n)\nSELECT any(n), anyHeavy(n) FROM cte\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─any(n)─┬─anyHeavy(n)─┐\n│      2 │           1 │\n└────────┴─────────────┘\n```\n\n## anyLast\n\nSelects the last encountered non-`NULL` value from a column within a group. The order of selection is non-deterministic unless the data is explicitly ordered.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\nanyLast(column) [RESPECT NULLS]\n```\n\nAlias: `last_value`.\nFor `anyLast(column) RESPECT NULLS`: `anyLast_respect_nulls`, `last_value_respect_nulls`.\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `column`: The column from which to select a value.\n\n### Returns\n\nThe last value encountered. If no non-`NULL` values are found, it returns the default value for the column's type. If `RESPECT NULLS` is used, it returns the last value encountered, even if it's `NULL`. The return type matches the input column's type.\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nWITH cte AS (SELECT arrayJoin([NULL, 'Amsterdam', 'New York', 'Tokyo', 'Valencia', NULL]) as city)\nSELECT anyLast(city), anyLast_respect_nulls(city) FROM cte\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─anyLast(city)─┬─anyLast_respect_nulls(city)─┐\n│ Valencia      │ ᴺᵁᴸᴸ                        │\n└───────────────┴─────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## approx_top_k\n\nReturns an array of the approximately most frequent values and their counts from a column. The result is sorted by approximate frequency in descending order.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\napprox_top_k(N)(column)\napprox_top_k(N, reserved)(column)\n```\n\nAlias: `approx_top_count`\n\n### Parameters\n\n- `N`: The maximum number of elements to return. Optional. Default: 10.\n- `reserved`: The number of cells reserved for values. If the number of unique values exceeds `reserved`, the result becomes approximate. Optional. Default: `N * 3`.\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `column`: The column whose value frequency is to be calculated.\n\n### Returns\n\nAn array of tuples, where each tuple contains a value, its approximate count, and an error value. Array(Tuple(T, UInt64, UInt64)).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT approx_top_k(2)(k)\nFROM values('k String, w UInt64', ('y', 1), ('y', 1), ('x', 5), ('y', 1), ('z', 10))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─approx_top_k(2)(k)────┐\n│ [('y',3,0),('x',1,0)] │\n└───────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## approx_top_sum\n\nReturns an array of the approximately most frequent values and their weighted counts from a column. The result is sorted by approximate weighted frequency in descending order.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\napprox_top_sum(N)(column, weight)\napprox_top_sum(N, reserved)(column, weight)\n```\n\n### Parameters\n\n- `N`: The maximum number of elements to return. Optional. Default: 10.\n- `reserved`: The number of cells reserved for values. If the number of unique values exceeds `reserved`, the result becomes approximate. Optional. Default: `N * 3`.\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `column`: The column whose value frequency is to be calculated.\n- `weight`: A `UInt64` value representing the weight for each `column` entry.\n\n### Returns\n\nAn array of tuples, where each tuple contains a value, its approximate weighted count, and an error value. Array(Tuple(T, UInt64, UInt64)).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT approx_top_sum(2)(k, w)\nFROM values('k String, w UInt64', ('y', 1), ('y', 1), ('x', 5), ('y', 1), ('z', 10))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─approx_top_sum(2)(k, w)─┐\n│ [('z',10,0),('x',5,0)]  │\n└─────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## argMax\n\nRetrieves the `arg` value associated with the maximum `val` value within a group. If multiple `val` values are tied for the maximum, the returned `arg` is non-deterministic. `NULL` values in `arg` or `val` are skipped.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\nargMax(arg, val)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `arg`: The argument whose value is to be returned.\n- `val`: The value used to determine the maximum.\n\n### Returns\n\nThe `arg` value corresponding to the maximum `val`. The return type matches the `arg` type.\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nWITH salary_data AS (\n  SELECT user, salary FROM values('user String, salary UInt32', ('director', 5000), ('manager', 3000), ('worker', 1000))\n)\nSELECT argMax(user, salary) FROM salary_data\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─argMax(user, salary)─┐\n│ director             │\n└──────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## argMin\n\nRetrieves the `arg` value associated with the minimum `val` value within a group. If multiple `val` values are tied for the minimum, the returned `arg` is non-deterministic. `NULL` values in `arg` or `val` are skipped.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\nargMin(arg, val)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `arg`: The argument whose value is to be returned.\n- `val`: The value used to determine the minimum.\n\n### Returns\n\nThe `arg` value corresponding to the minimum `val`. The return type matches the `arg` type.\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nWITH salary_data AS (\n  SELECT user, salary FROM values('user String, salary UInt32', ('director', 5000), ('manager', 3000), ('worker', 1000))\n)\nSELECT argMin(user, salary) FROM salary_data\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─argMin(user, salary)─┐\n│ worker               │\n└──────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## avg\n\nCalculates the arithmetic mean (average) of a set of numeric values.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\navg(x)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `x`: A numeric expression (Integer, Float, or Decimal).\n\n### Returns\n\nThe arithmetic mean as a `Float64`. Returns `NaN` if the input set is empty.\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT avg(x) FROM values('x Int8', 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5)\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─avg(x)─┐\n│    2.5 │\n└────────┘\n```\n\n## avgWeighted\n\nCalculates the weighted arithmetic mean of a set of values, where each value contributes according to its specified weight.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\navgWeighted(x, weight)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `x`: The numeric values (Integer or Float).\n- `weight`: The numeric weights corresponding to each value (Integer or Float).\n\n### Returns\n\nThe weighted arithmetic mean as a `Float64`. Returns `NaN` if all weights are zero or the input set is empty.\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT avgWeighted(x, w)\nFROM values('x Int8, w Int8', (4, 1), (1, 0), (10, 2))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─avgWeighted(x, weight)─┐\n│                      8 │\n└────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## boundingRatio\n\nCalculates the slope of the line connecting the leftmost and rightmost points in a series of (x, y) coordinates.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\nboundingRatio(x, y)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `x`: The x-coordinate values.\n- `y`: The y-coordinate values.\n\n### Returns\n\nThe slope as a `Float64`.\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT boundingRatio(number, number * 1.5)\nFROM numbers(10)\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─boundingRatio(number, multiply(number, 1.5))─┐\n│                                          1.5 │\n└──────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## categoricalInformationValue\n\nCalculates the Information Value (IV) for categorical features against a binary target variable. This metric helps assess the predictive power of categorical variables.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ncategoricalInformationValue(category1, category2, ..., tag)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `category1, category2, ...`: One or more categorical columns to analyze.\n- `tag`: The binary target variable (ee.g., 0 or 1).\n\n### Returns\n\nA `Float64` representing the Information Value.\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT categoricalInformationValue(category, target)\nFROM values('category String, target UInt8', ('A', 0), ('A', 1), ('B', 0), ('B', 0), ('C', 1))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─categoricalInformationValue(category, target)─┐\n│                                           0.0 │\n└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## contingency\n\nCalculates the contingency coefficient, a measure of association between two categorical variables. The coefficient ranges from 0 to 1, where higher values indicate a stronger association.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ncontingency(column1, column2)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `column1`: The first categorical column.\n- `column2`: The second categorical column.\n\n### Returns\n\nA `Float64` value between 0 and 1.\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    contingency(a ,b)\nFROM\n    (\n        SELECT\n            number % 10 AS a,\n            number % 4 AS b\n        FROM\n            numbers(150)\n    )\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─contingency(a, b)─┐\n│ 0.05812725261759165 │\n└───────────────────┘\n```\n\n## corr\n\nCalculates the Pearson correlation coefficient between two numeric variables. This function uses a numerically unstable algorithm.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ncorr(x, y)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `x`: The first numeric variable (Integer, Float, or Decimal).\n- `y`: The second numeric variable (Integer, Float, or Decimal).\n\n### Returns\n\nThe Pearson correlation coefficient as a `Float64`.\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT corr(c2, c3)\nFROM values((1, 5.6, -4.4),(2, -9.6, 3),(3, -1.3, -4),(4, 5.3, 9.7),(5, 4.4, 0.037),(6, -8.6, -7.8),(7, 5.1, 9.3),(8, 7.9, -3.6),(9, -8.2, 0.62),(10, -3, 7.3))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─corr(c2, c3)──────────┐\n│    0.1730265755453256 │\n└───────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## corrMatrix\n\nComputes the correlation matrix for a set of numeric variables.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ncorrMatrix(x[, ...])\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `x`: One or more numeric variables (Integer, Float, or Decimal).\n\n### Returns\n\nA nested array representing the correlation matrix. Array(Array(Float64)).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n  arrayMap(x -\u003e round(x, 3), arrayJoin(corrMatrix(c1, c2, c3, c4))) AS corrMatrix\nFROM values ((1, 5.6, -4.4, 2.6), (2, -9.6, 3, 3.3), (3, -1.3, -4, 1.2), (4, 5.3, 9.7, 2.3), (5, 4.4, 0.037, 1.222), (6, -8.6, -7.8, 2.1233), (7, 5.1, 9.3, 8.1222), (8, 7.9, -3.6, 9.837), (9, -8.2, 0.62, 8.43555), (10, -3, 7.3, 6.762))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\n┌─corrMatrix─────────────┐\n│ [1,-0.096,0.243,0.746] │\n│ [-0.096,1,0.173,0.106] │\n│ [0.243,0.173,1,0.258]  │\n│ [0.746,0.106,0.258,1]  │\n└────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## corrStable\n\nCalculates the Pearson correlation coefficient between two numeric variables using a numerically stable algorithm.
